raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX, a {$950.00} charge posted as XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX for a time-specific catering service for a live event. The merchant materially failed to provide the contracted service during the event window ( core items/quantities were missing ; the attempted correction occurred too late and the order still did not match what was paid for ). I attempted to resolve directly with the merchant on XX/XX/XXXX by phone ( approximately XXXX XXXX and XXXX XXXX  ). I also attempted to submit a complaint via the merchants website on XX/XX/XXXX ( submission error ) and attempted email contact on XX/XX/XXXX ( message returned/undeliverable ). 

I opened a dispute with Citi ( Case ID XXXX ). Citi issued a conditional/provisional credit during the investigation and requested additional documentation, which I provided ( invoice/order confirmation, detailed timeline, call evidence, photos showing what was delivered vs. what was later obtained, and screenshots of failed contact attempts ). Citi repeatedly required me to provide a partial dollar amount for service not received because some food was physically delivered. I explained in writing that this was a time-specific service and the failure during the event defeated the essential purpose of the service ; therefore the value of services not received was the full amount. 

Citi ultimately denied the dispute, stating I received the services for which [ I ] contracted, and reversed the conditional credit ( final letter dated XX/XX/XXXX ). Citi did not provide the merchant representment/response or other documentation it relied on, did not identify the dispute classification/reason code used, and did not give a detailed rationale addressing the evidence submitted. I am requesting CFPB assistance to require Citi to provide the basis and supporting documentation for its determination and to reopen/review the dispute consistent with applicable card dispute standards for time-specific services.
